description: This pipeline imports an image, applies Cellpose to it and then outputs the probabilities. The main steps include importing the image, applying Cellpose, and outputting the probabilities.

68997ba1-f4a5-445c-b7c2-8e01a22cdafa.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
68997ba1-f4a5-445c-b7c2-8e01a22cdafa.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

9410cdab-1ebb-4a0d-83c3-3c52c9fc090e.name: ij1-roi-filter-statistics
9410cdab-1ebb-4a0d-83c3-3c52c9fc090e.description:
Filters the ROI list elements via statistics.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

9f3c6cf6-2a6e-4f31-9673-9b8831aac4b7.name: ij1-roi-to-rgb
9f3c6cf6-2a6e-4f31-9673-9b8831aac4b7.description:
Converts ROI lists to masks. The line and fill color is stored within the ROI themselves. This algorithm needs a reference image that provides the output sizes. If you do not have a reference image, you can use the unreferenced variant.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

2a245bc3-ddc7-475b-bc62-788d88a95afd.name: ij1-blur-gaussian2d
2a245bc3-ddc7-475b-bc62-788d88a95afd.description: Applies convolution with a Gaussian function for smoothing. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

961d3685-5557-4dc8-84f6-2c093cbfd0e9.name: ij1-color-blend-images
961d3685-5557-4dc8-84f6-2c093cbfd0e9.description:
Overlays greyscale or RGB images.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot.

a3f69a70-df7a-4eda-9d33-43ee96e3dad1.name: ij1-analyze-find-particles2d
a3f69a70-df7a-4eda-9d33-43ee96e3dad1.description: Converts mask images into ROI by applying a connected components algorithm and generates measurements. Please note that this algorithm will always trace the external object boundaries and convert them into polygonal ROIs. This means that holes will be closed. This node requires a thresholded image as input and will extract measurements from the thresholded image. If you already have ROI available and want measurements, use 'Extract ROI statistics'.If higher-dimensional data is provided, the results are generated for each 2D slice.

513220be-0f29-4363-82dd-603b19e1092c.name: ij1-morph-operation2d
513220be-0f29-4363-82dd-603b19e1092c.description:
Applies a morphological operation to greyscale images. Following operations are included: Erosion, Dilation, Opening, Closing, White Top Hat, Black Top Hat, Gradient, Laplacian, Internal Gradient, and External Gradient. More information (including examples) can be found at https://imagej.net/MorphoLibJ.html
If a multi-channel image is provided, the operation is applied to each channel. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.

4959e8a3-8852-46d4-91c8-8abbeaab7dd9.name: cellpose-segmentation-inference-3.x
4959e8a3-8852-46d4-91c8-8abbeaab7dd9.description:
Runs Cellpose on the input image with the given model(s). This node supports both segmentation in 3D and executing Cellpose for each 2D image plane. This node can generate a multitude of outputs, although only ROI is activated by default. Go to the 'Outputs' parameter section to enable the other outputs.
  • Labels: A grayscale image where each connected component is assigned a unique value.
  • Flows XY: An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel
  • Flows Z: Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)
  • Flows d: Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)
  • Probabilities: An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel
  • ROI: ROI of the segmented areas.
Please note that you need to setup a valid Python environment with Cellpose installed. You can find the setting in Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose.

This algorithm merges all annotations and data annotations. Use the iteration step settings to determine how annotations and data annotations are merged

f16dcc7e-75da-4671-a665-f7870fe9fcac.name: cellpose-segmentation-inference-3.x
f16dcc7e-75da-4671-a665-f7870fe9fcac.description:

16856f19-8ea7-4dd7-9f55-0ab1c14a6509.name: ij1-background-rollingball2d
16856f19-8ea7-4dd7-9f55-0ab1c14a6509.description: Finds or subtracts the background of a 2D slice via a rolling-ball or paraboloid algorithm. If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice.
